root_text.an5.17:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an5.17:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attanā sīlasampanno hoti, no paraṁ sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.17:1.4: attanā samādhisampanno hoti, no paraṁ samādhisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.17:1.5: attanā paññāsampanno hoti, no paraṁ paññāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.17:1.6: attanā vimuttisampanno hoti, no paraṁ vimuttisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.17:1.7: attanā vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, no paraṁ vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadāya samādapeti.
root_text.an5.17:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti.

translation_text.an5.17:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is practicing for their own welfare, but not that of others.
translation_text.an5.17:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is personally accomplished in ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, and the knowledge and vision of freedom. But they don’t encourage others in these qualities.
translation_text.an5.17:1.8: A mendicant with these five qualities is practicing for their own welfare, but not that of others.”

reference_text.an5.17:1.1: csp1ed16.11, csp2ed16.11, dr22.14, ms16A5_63, msdiv17, pts-vp-pli3.13, vri37.12
